sections.label: Technique
sections.placeholder: Real-time greyscale and colour/spectral Doppler ultrasound of the female pelvis. State transducer and approach (e.g. transabdominal/endocavitary).

sections.label: Comparison
sections.placeholder: Date and type of relevant prior imaging, if available.

sections.label: Findings
sections.placeholder:
Uterus: size (3 dimensions), position/version, myometrium and any fibroids (number, location, size); adenomyosis features.
Endometrium: thickness (double-layer, mm), echogenicity, cavity fluid; correlate with menopausal status.
Cervix.
Right ovary: size/volume, follicles, any lesion. Left ovary: same.
Adnexal lesion characterisation (O-RADS US v2022 lexicon, optimised on high-resolution transvaginal imaging): maximum diameter (mm), unilocular vs multilocular, smooth vs irregular wall/septa, solid component, number of papillary projections, acoustic shadowing, colour Doppler score (1-4), and assigned O-RADS US category 1-5.
Free fluid / cul-de-sac; ascites or peritoneal nodularity.
Bladder and visualised bowel.
Lymph nodes if seen.

sections.label: Impression
sections.placeholder: Concise prioritised bullets. For each adnexal lesion give the O-RADS US category (1-5), laterality, and maximum diameter (mm). Management implication: O-RADS 2 (almost certainly benign, <1%) = benign, follow-up only per protocol/symptoms; O-RADS 3 (low, 1-<10%) = US follow-up or consider MRI; O-RADS 4 (intermediate, 10-<50%) = MRI characterisation and/or gynaecology referral; O-RADS 5 (high, >=50%) = refer to gynaecological oncology MDT. Note endometrial thickness relative to menopausal status. Use only approved follow-up phrasing.
